steps.title: Identify the three interior angles
steps.content: The triangle has three interior angles: one measuring 31°, one measuring $2b°$, and one measuring $a°$. We're given that $a = 45$, so the third angle is \$45°$.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property and solve for b
steps.content:
The sum of all interior angles in any triangle is 180°. Setting up the equation:

$$31 + 2b + 45 = 180$$

Combine the constants:

$$76 + 2b = 180$$

Subtract 76 from both sides:

$$2b = 104$$

Divide by 2:

$$b = 52$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
